skill.name: receiving-code-review
skill.description: Guides AI coding assistants through careful, evidence-based responses to code review feedback before making changes.
skill.summary: Evaluate code review feedback with technical rigor before implementing changes.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backtick fences as shell execution, plus weak cryptography and network reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found no executable code, no network behavior, no cryptographic logic, and no prompt injection attempt in the single SKILL.md file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown fenced examples and process checklists, not Ruby or shell command execution. No executable script or command invocation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced Markdown blocks that describe review behavior. They do not contain executable Ruby backticks, shell commands, subprocess APIs, or script files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections point to prose and example response text. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage appears at the reported lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter description text, and line 135 is a sample review response. Neither line contains cryptographic behavior or code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance detection points to guidance about using technical reasoning during code review. No network scanning, host discovery, DNS lookup, or outbound connection behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported line is plain Markdown prose: it advises technical reasoning. There is no network-related command or API call.

content.value_statement: Code review feedback can be unclear, incomplete, or technically wrong for the current codebase. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code verify feedback before changing code.
actual_capabilities: Structures responses to review feedback into reading, understanding, verifying, evaluating, responding, and implementing.
Encourages clarification before implementing unclear multi-item feedback.
Distinguishes trusted partner feedback from external reviewer suggestions.
Prompts technical pushback when a suggestion conflicts with codebase facts.
Prioritizes blocking issues, simple fixes, complex fixes, and regression checks.
Discourages performative agreement and replaces it with concrete technical action.
limitations: Does not perform code review or security analysis by itself.
Does not include scripts, tools, or automated checks.
Requires access to the relevant codebase to verify reviewer claims.
May need human direction for architectural or product decisions.

use_cases.title: Clarify Ambiguous Review Items
use_cases.description: Pause implementation and ask targeted questions when some review items are unclear.

use_cases.title: Evaluate External Suggestions
use_cases.description: Check whether reviewer feedback fits the repository, platform support, and existing behavior.

use_cases.title: Implement Review Fixes Safely
use_cases.description: Apply confirmed changes in a clear order and verify each fix before moving on.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Review Triage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feedback, restate each requested change, and identify anything that needs clarification before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Check Technical Validity
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate these review comments against the current codebase. Mark each item as confirmed, unclear, or technically questionable.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Safe Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation order for this review feedback. Prioritize blocking issues, simple fixes, complex fixes, and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Reasoned Pushback
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a concise technical response for review comments that appear incorrect, incomplete, or inconsistent with existing architecture.

output_examples.input: Reviewer says to remove legacy support, but the repository still targets older systems.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks platform requirements, identifies the compatibility conflict, and asks whether to keep support or update the target policy.

output_examples.input: A six-item review has two unclear requests.
output_examples.output: The assistant summarizes the four understood items and asks for clarification before implementing any of the set.

output_examples.input: Reviewer requests a new reporting feature that may not be used.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches for usage evidence, reports that no caller was found, and asks whether to remove or implement the feature.

best_practices: Verify every material review suggestion against repository facts before editing code.
Ask clear questions when feedback has missing context or unclear scope.
Test each confirmed change before starting the next review item.
anti_patterns: Implementing review feedback before understanding the requested change.
Accepting external suggestions without checking existing behavior.
Batching unrelated fixes without verification between changes.

faq.question: Does this skill write code fixes automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides how an assistant should evaluate review feedback before making code changes.

faq.question: Can it reject reviewer feedback?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports concise technical pushback when evidence shows a suggestion is wrong or risky.

faq.question: Does it need repository access?
faq.answer: Repository access is needed for verification, tests, and checking whether suggested changes are actually used.

faq.question: Is this useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow applies to Claude, Codex, and Claude Code during review-driven coding tasks.

faq.question: Does it include automated security tooling?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guidance skill and does not include scripts, scanners, or network calls.

faq.question: When should a human be involved?
faq.answer: A human should decide when feedback conflicts with architecture, product scope, or prior project decisions.
